title: Herpetospermum darjeelingense

The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its leaves and roots are valued for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate pain, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Herpetospermum darjeelingense include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, skin disorders, and respiratory infections.
